title: Milestone Lens Navigator
prompt:
Create a production-ready Milestone Lens Navigator using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original navigation pattern presents three delivery milestones as a compact horizontal lens; choosing a milestone moves an outlined lens to that step and reveals a concise status below it. Build it for a light Bento interface with a white panel on #F4F5F6, 22px corners, #ECECEE border, soft shadow, #111111 heading text, and #4A4A4F secondary text. Use sky blue as the single bright accent and avoid card gradients or decorative clutter.

Use a semantic tablist with three real tab buttons, aria-selected, aria-controls, and a matching tabpanel. Tabs must have 44px targets and support click, Enter, Space, Home, End, and arrow-key navigation. Start on the Build milestone. The lens should move with a stiffness 310, damping 25 spring; panel copy should fade over 160ms ease-out, while press feedback lasts 90ms. Keep state local and favor opacity and transform. Do not use timers, random values, storage, network requests, remote assets, or page-level positioning.

At 320px, preserve readable labels by allowing the rail to scroll or wrap without clipping; at wider sizes, use the available horizontal space. Provide a visible dark focus ring, logical tab order, AA contrast, and a text status that does not rely on color. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by placing the lens immediately and using a brief opacity fade only. Return full paste-ready client code, followed by an executable pure CSS approximation and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML: <nav class="milestone-lens"><button aria-selected="true">Build</button></nav> */
.milestone-lens{display:flex;gap:4px;padding:4px;border-radius:16px;background:#f4f5f6}.milestone-lens button{flex:1;min-height:44px;border:0;border-radius:12px;background:transparent;color:#111;font-weight:800}.milestone-lens button[aria-selected=true]{border:2px solid #111;background:#fff}.milestone-lens button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.milestone-lens *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
